steps.title: Identify the turning point
steps.content: The passage initially frames probabilistic models and causal determinism as seemingly incompatible. The key signal is the final sentence's pivot: “But complete foreknowledge of relevant conditions is generally unavailable.” This reveals that our epistemic limitation—not any genuine logical conflict—accounts for the apparent incompatibility.

steps.title: Match the resolution to the choices
steps.content: Choice A directly states the resolution: causal determinism doesn't require absolute certainty—outcomes can be determined by conditions while remaining unpredictable from our perspective of incomplete knowledge. The other choices either deny any reconciliation (B), emphasize past data (C), or impose an unwarranted requirement about overwhelming probability (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
